LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. NO STATUTORY BASIS SUPPORTS THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. NO SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP EXISTS BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS. NO LEGAL DUTY IS OWED TO PLAINTIFF FOR THE ALLEGED MISCONDUCT. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2012 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Off Calendar Dec-08-2011 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2011 Continued To Dec-15-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Compromise Of Pending Action For An Adult With Disability.
Off Calendar Oct-24-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on Plaintiffs' Motion for Apportionment and for an Order to Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 218, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Brenda Fields, #7235.

Gregory Winslow, Esq. and Jerry Hurtubise, Esq. for Petitioner Wanda F. Clifton as Guardian Ad Litem for the Disable Adult Claimant David W. Wheeler.
Keven Urbatsch, Esq. for Conservator Wanda F. Clifton and for the Qualify Settlement Funds.
David AQ. Delbon, Esq. for Defendant C.C.S.F.

After hearing testimony and receiving evidence by counsel and Ms. Clifton, matter was argued and taken under submission for further review.

10-24-11
On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved.
Settlement of $5,360,000.00
Attorney's fees $1,608,000.00
Costs $214782.62
Net Balance $3,198,842.62
Orders signed by Judge Chaitin.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Gregory F. Winslow, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 621-2131 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; David A. Delbon, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3962 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action. No statutory basis supports this cause of action. No special relationship exists between plaintiff and defendants. No legal duty is owed to plaintiff for the alleged misconduct. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must specifically allege in the second cause of action the statutory basis for a separate negligence claim against CCSF. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189
